metadata.greeting: Get ready for a weekend of fun! I've found some fantastic activities perfect for a 3-year-old in Hackney, including puppet shows, museum visits, and outdoor adventures. Let's make this weekend memorable!

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Explore the newly transformed Young V&A, co-designed with children. It features three permanent gallery spaces called Imagine, Play and Design with interactive displays and an Open Studio.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for children with hands-on exhibits, sensory materials and a Mini Museum for under 3s.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green. It's a one-minute walk from the station.
transport.tip: The nearest tube station, Bethnal Green, does not have step-free access.

activities.title: A Squash and a Squeeze
activities.description: Play based on the original book A Squash and a Squeeze, written by Julia Donaldson and illustrated by Axel Scheffler.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppets and a well-known story will engage a 3 year old.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then a short bus ride.

activities.title: The Everywhere Bear
activities.description: Stage adaptation of Julia Donaldson's story brought to life with rhyme, original music and delightfully expressive puppets.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppets and music will engage a 3 year old.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then a short bus ride.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: Small and beautifully designed wooden playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Local, free and designed for toddlers.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Explore London Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the park.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines animals and an Easter egg hunt, great for a 3-year-old.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Euston, then a short bus ride to the Zoo.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A pirate ship wooden playground with engaging play areas for kids of all ages.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a wide range of play equipment and is located in a beautiful park.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Notting Hill Gate, then a short walk through Kensington Gardens.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.price: Included with entry to the ship
activities.description: Take part in an Easter Egg Trail throughout the historic ship. Discover fascinating facts about Cutty Sark's epic voyages, while you explore the decks — and keep your eyes peeled for the hidden eggs.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun Easter activity combined with exploring a famous ship.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Cutty Sark station.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny Statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor activity with a chocolate reward, perfect for burning energy.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Liverpool Street, then a train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Go early in the day to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Octonauts Above & Beyond
activities.description: Enjoy a fun-filled family Easter adventure where 3-6s explore a series of installations to find the bees, build them a new hive and help get them there safely.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive trail suitable for the age range in a beautiful outdoor setting.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Richmond, then a bus to Kew Gardens.

activities.title: Fairy Tales
activities.description: Explore enchanted lands, magical creatures, iconic characters and timeless tales, brought to life through books, theatre costumes, puppets, pop-ups, artwork and illustrations.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and tactile exhibition that explores enchanted lands, magical creatures, iconic characters and timeless tales.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then a Victoria Line to Euston.
